channel.title: Posts on srvrlss.dev
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on srvrlss.dev

item.title: Token Exhaustion: What Three AI Coding Agents Taught Me in a Single Week
item.description:
I use GitHub Copilot. Curiosity pulled me toward Antigravity and Claude Code. I had a few experiments in mind. This was the perfect chance. My plan was simple: I would jump from one tool to the next and experience their workflows firsthand. The token wall blindsided me.

Token Wall: You run out of credits or have reached the productivity window usage limit.

The Token Wall: A Week of Surprises

First, GitHub Copilot. Next, Antigravity, with my Gemini Pro subscription. Finally, Claude Code on a brand new Pro account. Three AI coding agents. One week.

item.title: The New Waste in AI Engineering: When Assumptions Age Faster Than Code
item.description:
The New Waste Is Skipping Research & Planning

In 2026, the most expensive mistake in AI engineering is treating a fast-moving assumption like a stable fact.

One prompt, one agent away, generating code has become so cheap that I skipped the research phase entirely. I learned this the hard way while building a tool to run Gemma 4 on Apple Silicon. My intuition defaulted to execution because building felt faster than searching. The real cost was the hours and tokens lost by skipping a ten-minute check. I found that official support already existed. My work was sound and my premise was stale.
